article_title: Eligible market requirements for Market Authorizations
body: Issue date: July 19, 2024 These changes apply to the following licence: Liquor manufacturers that want to sell at an artisan, farmers or annual market must make sure the market is eligible. Previously, markets needed to be composedentirelyof vendors who sell artisanal goods of their own making, and or agricultural goods that they produce, grow or raise themselves. Now, eligible markets will only requiremore than half of all vendorsin the following categories combined, to be present on a typical market day: If the licensee already has a market authorization, no action is required to conduct sales and sampling at markets. Learn more aboutLiquor sales at artisan, farmers and annual markets. Read all about the changes in the: It is the licensee’s responsibility to coordinate with the market to ensure that all eligibility criteria are met. The Manufacturer terms and conditions contain the complete list of market eligibility criteria. If the licensee does not yet have a market authorization and would like to apply for one, they must also have anon-site store endorsement. Licensees can apply through theliquor and cannabis licensing portal. There is no deadline or expiry date. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: BC liquor makers: market eligibility rule relaxed for artisan and farmers markets
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has changed the eligibility rules for liquor manufacturers who want to sell or sample their products at artisan, farmers, or annual markets. Previously, every vendor at the market had to sell only goods they made or grew themselves. Now, only more than half of the vendors need to meet that requirement. If you already have a market authorization, you don't need to do anything. If you want a new one, you must also have an on-site store endorsement.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you do not yet have a market authorization, apply through the BC liquor and cannabis licensing portal after getting an on-site store endorsement.
